overview.description: A naturally sweet, soft fruit with a creamy texture and a mild flavour.

learning.did-you-know: Bananas are technically berries, while strawberries are not. Botanically, a berry is a fruit that develops from a single flower with one ovary and usually contains seeds inside the flesh.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How bananas change as they ripen and why their flavour and texture become sweeter and softer over time.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A simple, convenient fruit snack that requires no cooking and gives children an opportunity to learn about the food they are eating.
chapman-tips: Green bananas are firmer and less sweet.
Yellow bananas are usually sweet and ready to eat.
Brown spots generally indicate increased ripeness and sweetness.
Very ripe bananas are excellent for baking.
notes.leftovers: Overripe bananas can be used in banana bread, smoothies, pancakes or frozen for later use.
